title: maba piscatoria
intro: Maba piscatoria, also known as the fish poison tree, is a plant species native to tropical Asia and Australia. It contains a toxic compound called mabagenin, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Maba piscatoria include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, Maba piscatoria is often used in traditional remedies, including teas, infusions, and poultices, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity.
